title: Acceptance Contract Completion Record
description: Per-run completion record written to .taskmaster/contract/<run-id>.completion.json. Validated by scripts/contract/validate_completion.py, the single component that decides whether a record may claim PASS. run_id is the only unconditionally-required field: a signed-skip record legitimately carries no verifier results, so requiring criteria_results/readiness_verdict here would misclassify a valid skip as a schema violation. Deeper certification requirements (freeze evidence, chokepoint token, tier-3 sign-off) are enforced by the validator's logic, not by required-ness in this schema.

run_id.description: Run identifier: a Task Master tag for /tm, an issue-queue slug for /issues. Keys every contract artifact for the run.

contract_hash.description: sha256 of the frozen contract file, recorded at freeze before decomposition. The exit verifier re-hashes and aborts on mismatch (C5).

freeze_evidence.description: Present iff the contract was frozen (hash recorded before decomposition, kill-test passed). Absence means the run was skipped or never froze; the validator caps it accordingly and it can never certify PASS.

kill_test.description: B1/B2 kill-test evidence: the null artifact failed every criterion (and the sabotage was rejected where authored).

readiness_verdict.description: A2/A3 readiness check verdict and the decorrelation source. source: none stamps the run DEGRADED (no decorrelated review).

criteria_results.description: Per-criterion observed results from the cold-exit verifier. Written only via the chokepoint (C4).

tier2_calibration.description: C2 calibration record proving a tier-2 gate caught a planted regression. No v1 task seeds this, so tier-2 blocking is unarmed in v1: absent calibration means tier-2 reports, never blocks.

couldnt_drive.description: C3: every criterion the verifier could not execute. Non-empty => PARTIAL, never PASS.

stamps.description: Verifier/validator stamps recorded on the run (DEGRADED / DEGRADED-custody / PARTIAL / UNVERIFIED / contract-skipped). The validator recomputes these; a record may pre-declare them.

abort_events.description: D2: raw record of aborts (e.g. mid-run contract-hash mismatch, C5). Non-empty => the run aborted and cannot certify.

tier3_escalations.description: C2 tier-3 escalations to the operator, each with a human-observable artifact path. The run does not certify until operator_signoff is recorded.

awaiting_signoff.description: Honest per-entry marker: true until the operator's sign-off is recorded. Certification is gated on record-level operator_signoff (C2, criterion 7), not on this flag.

verifier_provenance.description: C4 detection half: the chokepoint's per-run random token, echoed here. The validator requires it to exactly match the provenance side-channel entry for THIS run; absent/mismatched/stale => DEGRADED-custody, no PASS.

operator_signoff.description: Operator authorization: a signed skip (A1, caps the run at UNVERIFIED) or a tier-3 sign-off (certifies an otherwise-complete run). Presence (non-null) means signed.
